verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 is a secreted cysteine-rich protein expressed in the epididymis and secreted into the epididymal lumen. It binds to the sperm surface — specifically the postacrosomal/equatorial segment — and participates in sperm-egg fusion. The protein is not a transmembrane or GPI-anchored protein by its own architecture; it is secreted and then associates with the sperm plasma membrane surface in a stable, wash-resistant manner during epididymal transit. This constitutes a tissue/cell-type-restricted surface display (restricted to mature spermatozoa in the male reproductive tract) via stable post-translational attachment to the sperm PM. This is not a reversible, equilibrium-based recruitment — the protein is stably bound to the sperm surface after epididymal coating. Verdict: contextual / tissue_restricted_surface, as surface display is restricted to a narrow germline/reproductive lineage (spermatozoa).
predicted_key_uncertainty: Precise wash-resistance and anchoring mechanism of CRISP1 on the sperm surface is not fully characterized; unclear if binding is covalent or merely very tight non-covalent.
